41.title: Buying a ticket — key phrases
41.intro: Today you learn to buy a ticket and navigate a Croatian bus or train station. The pattern 'karta za + accusative' (a ticket to...) plus a handful of station words (polazak, dolazak, peron, kolodvor) covers almost every ticket-window conversation.

items.note: karta za + city in the accusative

items.en: When does the bus to Split leave?
From which platform does it leave?
How much does a ticket cost?
How long does the ride take?

41.title: At the ticket window
41.intro: Practise the ticket-buying phrases: asking about departures, platforms and prices.

questions.prompt: How do you ask when the bus to Split leaves?
questions.explanation: 'Kada polazi...?' asks about departure time; 'gdje' asks where, 'koliko košta' asks about price.

questions.prompt: Jednu ___ za Zagreb, molim. (a ticket)
questions.explanation: 'karta' is the object of the request, so it takes the accusative: kartu.

questions.prompt: What does 'povratna karta' mean?
questions.explanation: povratna karta = return ticket; jednosmjerna karta = one-way ticket.

questions.prompt: S kojeg ___ polazi vlak? (platform)
questions.explanation: After 's kojeg' (from which) the noun takes the genitive: peron → perona.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'When does the next bus to Split leave?'
questions.answer: Kada polazi sljedeći autobus za Split?
questions.explanation: Question word first, then the verb: Kada polazi sljedeći autobus za Split?

questions.prompt: Where do you buy a train ticket?
questions.explanation: željeznički kolodvor = train station; ljekarna = pharmacy, pekara = bakery.

questions.prompt: Autobus polazi u osam, a ___ u Split je u podne. (arrival)
questions.explanation: polazak = departure, dolazak = arrival.

questions.prompt: The clerk says: 'Peron broj dva.' What did you learn?
options: The bus leaves from platform two.
The bus leaves at two o'clock.
questions.answer: The bus leaves from platform two.
questions.explanation: peron = platform, broj dva = number two.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'How much does a return ticket to Rijeka cost?'
questions.answer: Koliko košta povratna karta za Rijeku?
questions.explanation: Koliko košta + subject: Koliko košta povratna karta za Rijeku?

41.title: Buying a bus ticket to Split
41.intro: You are at the bus station in Zagreb and want a return ticket to Split. Read your lines aloud, then cover the Croatian and reconstruct them from the English.

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Dobar dan. Jednu kartu za Split, molim.
lines.en: Good day. One ticket to Split, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar dan. Jednosmjernu ili povratnu?
lines.en: Good day. One-way or return?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Povratnu, molim. Koliko košta?
lines.en: Return, please. How much does it cost?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Povratna karta košta trideset eura.
lines.en: A return ticket costs thirty euros.

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: U redu. Kada polazi sljedeći autobus?
lines.en: All right. When does the next bus leave?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Polazi u devet i trideset, s perona broj četiri.
lines.en: It leaves at nine thirty, from platform number four.

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: A kada stiže u Split?
lines.en: And when does it arrive in Split?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Stiže u četrnaest sati. Vožnja traje oko četiri i pol sata.
lines.en: It arrives at 2 p.m. The ride takes about four and a half hours.

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Izvrsno. Izvolite karticu.
lines.en: Excellent. Here is my card.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hvala. Izvolite kartu i račun. Sretan put!
lines.en: Thank you. Here is your ticket and receipt. Have a good trip!

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Hvala lijepa. Doviđenja!
lines.en: Thank you very much. Goodbye!

42.title: The instrumental — how and with whom
42.intro: The instrumental case answers 'with whom?' and 'by what means?'. Endings: masculine and neuter take -om (vlak → vlakom), or -em after a soft consonant (č, ć, đ, ž, š, j, lj, nj: tramvaj → tramvajem, prijatelj → prijateljem); feminine -a becomes -om (sestra → sestrom). Use s/sa ONLY for company ('with a friend' = s prijateljem); for means of transport there is NO preposition: putujem vlakom, never 's vlakom'. Before s, š, z, ž (and in 'sa mnom'), s becomes sa.

items.note: no preposition for means of transport!
an adverb — no case ending needed
s → sa before s, š, z, ž

items.en: I travel to Split by train.
I am going to the cinema with a friend.

42.title: Instrumental transformations
42.intro: Turn nouns into the instrumental and choose when to use s/sa — and when to leave it out.

questions.prompt: Putujem ___ u Rijeku. (vlak)
questions.explanation: Means of transport: instrumental with no preposition — vlak → vlakom.

questions.prompt: Idem na posao ___. (autobus)
questions.explanation: autobus + -om = autobusom (by bus).

questions.prompt: How do you say 'by tram'?
questions.explanation: After the soft consonant j the ending is -em, and means of transport takes no preposition.

questions.prompt: Idem u kino s ___. (prijatelj)
questions.explanation: lj is a soft consonant, so prijatelj takes -em: s prijateljem.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'with my sister'?
questions.explanation: s becomes sa before s, š, z, ž; sestra → sestrom in the instrumental.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'I travel by car'?
questions.explanation: Means of transport = bare instrumental. 's autom' would mean the car is your travel companion!

questions.prompt: Pijem kavu s ___. (mlijeko)
questions.explanation: Neuter mlijeko takes -om: kava s mlijekom = coffee with milk.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Tomorrow I am travelling to Zagreb by bus.'
questions.explanation: Time word first, then verb, means (autobusom) and destination (u Zagreb).

questions.prompt: Ideš li ___ u grad? (with me)
questions.explanation: 'With me' is always 'sa mnom' — s becomes sa before the pronoun form mnom.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I am going to the seaside with a (female) friend.'
questions.explanation: na more = to the seaside; s prijateljicom = with a friend (instrumental).

43.title: Reading: Moje putovanje u Split
43.intro: Read this short A1 text twice — first for the gist, then slowly. — MOJE PUTOVANJE U SPLIT: Zovem se Petra i živim u Zagrebu. Prošlog vikenda putovala sam u Split. Išla sam autobusom jer je karta jeftina. Autobus je polazio u sedam sati ujutro s glavnog kolodvora. Putovanje je trajalo pet sati. U Splitu sam šetala uz more i pila kavu s prijateljicom. Vrijeme je bilo sunčano i toplo. Navečer smo jele ribu u malom restoranu. Vratila sam se kući u nedjelju navečer. Bilo je predivno! — Now mine it: the phrases below are the ones worth stealing for your own stories.

items.note: genitive of time — a set phrase

items.hr: polazio je u sedam sati ujutro
items.en: it departed at seven in the morning
I drank coffee with a friend

items.hr: vrijeme je bilo sunčano i toplo
items.en: the weather was sunny and warm

43.title: Did you understand the text?
43.intro: Comprehension questions about Petra's trip, plus the best phrases from the text.

questions.prompt: Kamo je Petra putovala prošlog vikenda?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Prošlog vikenda putovala sam u Split.'

questions.prompt: Kako je Petra putovala?
questions.explanation: 'Išla sam autobusom' — she went by bus (instrumental of means).

questions.prompt: Zašto je išla autobusom?
questions.explanation: '...jer je karta jeftina' — because the ticket is cheap.

questions.prompt: Putovanje je ___ pet sati. (lasted)
questions.explanation: trajati = to last; neuter past form because 'putovanje' is neuter: trajalo.

questions.prompt: Što je Petra radila u Splitu?
options: Šetala je uz more i pila kavu.
questions.answer: Šetala je uz more i pila kavu.
questions.explanation: 'U Splitu sam šetala uz more i pila kavu s prijateljicom.'

questions.prompt: Vratila sam se ___ u nedjelju navečer. (home)
questions.explanation: 'kući' = home(wards) — a set adverbial form, no preposition needed.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'The weather was sunny and warm.'
questions.answer: Vrijeme je bilo sunčano i toplo.
questions.explanation: Subject + je + bilo (neuter, agreeing with vrijeme) + adjectives.

questions.prompt: What does 'predivno' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Bilo je predivno!' = It was wonderful!

questions.prompt: Pila sam kavu s ___. (prijateljica)
questions.explanation: s + instrumental: prijateljica → prijateljicom.

44.title: Week-review conversation starters
44.intro: Real conversations mix topics — travel, weather, health — without warning. These frames let you carry a ten-minute chat about your week using everything from days 36-43.

items.note: to a woman: Jesi li putovala negdje?

items.en: What are you planning for next week?

44.title: Week 6 mixed review
44.intro: Travel, instrumental, health and weather — all in one exercise, the way real life serves them.

questions.prompt: U subotu sam išao ___ u Rijeku. (vlak)
questions.explanation: Means of transport: instrumental without a preposition — vlakom.

questions.prompt: How do you ask a friend what the weather was like?
questions.explanation: kakvo = what kind of; 'Kakvo je bilo vrijeme?' = What was the weather like?

questions.prompt: Ujutro je ___ kiša, ali poslije je bilo sunčano. (padati, past)
questions.explanation: kiša is feminine, so the past participle is padala: Padala je kiša.

questions.prompt: What does 'Boljelo me grlo' mean?
questions.explanation: boljeti = to hurt; grlo = throat. Literally: 'The throat hurt me.'

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Now I feel much better.'
questions.explanation: The clitic 'se' goes in second position: Sada se osjećam...

questions.prompt: Kupio sam povratnu ___ za Split. (ticket)
questions.explanation: Direct object in the accusative: karta → kartu.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
options: Putovao sam s autobusom s prijateljem.
questions.explanation: Means = bare instrumental (autobusom); company = s + instrumental (s prijateljem).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'What are you planning for next week?'
questions.explanation: Question word + verb + za + accusative time phrase.

44.title: Catching up on the week
44.intro: A friendly conversation that hops between the week's themes: travel, weather, health and plans. Play your side aloud, then swap roles.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bok! Kako si? Kakav ti je bio tjedan?
lines.en: Hi! How are you? How was your week?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Bok! Dobro sam, hvala. Tjedan je bio zanimljiv — puno sam učio.
lines.en: Hi! I'm fine, thanks. The week was interesting — I studied a lot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Jesi li putovao negdje?
lines.en: Did you travel anywhere?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Da, u subotu sam išao vlakom u Rijeku s prijateljem.
lines.en: Yes, on Saturday I went to Rijeka by train with a friend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Super! Kakvo je bilo vrijeme?
lines.en: Great! What was the weather like?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Ujutro je padala kiša, ali poslije je bilo sunčano.
lines.en: In the morning it was raining, but later it was sunny.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A kako se osjećaš? Prošli tjedan si bio bolestan.
lines.en: And how are you feeling? Last week you were ill.

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Sada se osjećam puno bolje. Boljelo me grlo, ali više me ne boli.
lines.en: Now I feel much better. I had a sore throat, but it doesn't hurt any more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Drago mi je! Što planiraš za sljedeći tjedan?
lines.en: I'm glad! What are you planning for next week?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Želim više razgovarati na hrvatskom i naučiti nove riječi.
lines.en: I want to speak more Croatian and learn new words.

45.title: Expressing opinions
45.intro: Opinion frames let you say something about anything. Model sentence: 'Mislim da je Zagreb lijep grad jer ima puno parkova.' (I think Zagreb is a beautiful city because it has many parks.) Learn the frame, then swap in your own topic and reason.

items.note: the workhorse — always followed by a full clause
give a reason after every opinion

45.title: Opinions, agreement and polite disagreement
45.intro: Practise stating opinions, agreeing and disagreeing without starting a fight.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I think that...'?
questions.explanation: misliti = to think; 'Mislim da...' introduces an opinion.

questions.prompt: ___ se s tobom. (I agree)
questions.explanation: slagati se = to agree; Slažem se s tobom = I agree with you.

questions.prompt: What does 'po meni' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Po meni' literally 'by me' = in my opinion (informal).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I think that coffee in Croatia is very good.'
questions.answer: Mislim da je kava u Hrvatskoj jako dobra.
questions.explanation: After 'da' the clitic 'je' comes immediately: Mislim da je kava...

questions.prompt: Ne ___ se — po meni je vlak bolji od autobusa. (agree)
questions.explanation: Ne slažem se = I don't agree.

questions.prompt: Which is a POLITE way to disagree?
questions.explanation: 'Maybe, but I think differently' softens the disagreement; the other options are rude.

questions.prompt: Zašto tako ___? (misliti, ti)
questions.explanation: misliti in the ti-form: misliš. 'Zašto tako misliš?' = Why do you think so?

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'In my opinion, Split is the most beautiful city.'
questions.answer: Po mom mišljenju, Split je najljepši grad.
questions.explanation: Po mom mišljenju + subject + je + superlative adjective + noun.

questions.prompt: Your friend says 'Imaš pravo.' What did they just do?
options: They agreed that you are right.
They asked you to turn right.
They said you have the law.
questions.answer: They agreed that you are right.
questions.explanation: Imaš pravo / U pravu si = You're right.

46.title: The vocative — calling and addressing
46.intro: The vocative is the case for calling or addressing someone directly: 'Ivane, dođi!' Rules of thumb: masculine nouns in a hard consonant add -e (gospodin → gospodine!), soft-consonant nouns add -u (prijatelj → prijatelju!), feminine common nouns in -a switch to -o (gospođa → gospođo!), while most personal names like Marko, Ana or Ivana stay unchanged (Ivan → Ivane! is the classic exception). It is used ONLY for addressing — never as a subject or object.

items.note: most female names stay the same
k, g, h soften to č, ž, š — hence 'Bože moj!' (my God!)

46.title: Address people correctly
46.intro: Call friends, strangers and professionals with the right vocative form.

questions.prompt: You want to call your friend Ivan across the street. You shout:
questions.explanation: Masculine names in a hard consonant take -e in the vocative: Ivan → Ivane!

questions.prompt: Dobar dan, ___! (gospodin)
questions.explanation: gospodin → gospodine! (sir) — the standard polite address to a man.

questions.prompt: Oprostite, ___! (gospođa)
questions.explanation: Feminine common nouns in -a take -o: gospođa → gospođo!

questions.prompt: What is the vocative of 'Marko'?
questions.explanation: Names ending in -o stay unchanged in the vocative: Marko → Marko!

questions.prompt: ___, dođi ovamo! (prijatelj)
questions.explanation: After a soft consonant (lj) the vocative ending is -u: prijatelju!

questions.prompt: When do you use the vocative?
options: Only when addressing or calling someone directly.
For the subject of a sentence.
questions.answer: Only when addressing or calling someone directly.
questions.explanation: The vocative has one job: direct address. It never marks subjects or objects.

questions.prompt: Hvala vam, ___! (doktor)
questions.explanation: doktor → doktore! Hard consonant + -e.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'Mr Horvat, your ticket is here.'
questions.answer: Gospodine Horvat, vaša karta je ovdje.
questions.explanation: The title takes the vocative (gospodine), the surname stays unchanged.

questions.prompt: What does 'Bože moj!' mean?
questions.explanation: Bog → Bože! — g softens to ž in the vocative. A very common exclamation.

47.title: Storytelling with aspect
47.intro: A Croatian story runs on two engines: the imperfective paints the background (what was going on), the perfective drives the plot (what happened next). Model story: 'Dok sam jučer čekao tramvaj, čitao sam novine. Odjednom sam vidio staru prijateljicu. Popili smo kavu i dugo razgovarali. Na kraju smo se dogovorili za večeru.' Background = čekao, čitao; events = vidio, popili, dogovorili se.

items.en: to watch / to take a look
While I was waiting for the bus...

items.note: connectors that hold a story together

47.title: Background or event?
47.intro: Choose the right aspect: imperfective for the scenery, perfective for the plot.

questions.prompt: 'Dok sam ___ autobus, čitao sam novine.' (While I was waiting for the bus, I was reading the paper.)
questions.explanation: Ongoing background action after 'dok' → imperfective čekati: čekao.

questions.prompt: 'Jučer sam ___ cijelu knjigu.' (Yesterday I read the whole book — and finished it.)
questions.explanation: A completed result (the WHOLE book) → perfective pročitati: pročitao.

questions.prompt: Prvo sam ___ kavu, zatim sam otišao na posao. (popiti, past, m.)
questions.explanation: A finished event in a sequence → perfective popiti: popio sam kavu.

questions.prompt: Which verb is PERFECTIVE?
questions.explanation: kupiti = to buy (one completed act); kupovati and gledati are imperfective.

questions.prompt: Svaki dan ___ novine, ali jučer nisam imao vremena. (čitati, ja, present)
questions.explanation: Habitual action (every day) → imperfective present: čitam.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'While I was waiting for the tram, I saw a friend.'
questions.answer: Dok sam čekao tramvaj, vidio sam prijatelja.
questions.explanation: dok + imperfective background (čekao), then the perfective-style event (vidio sam).

questions.prompt: Which sentence means you FINISHED the letter?
questions.explanation: Perfective napisati = the letter got written; pisao sam = I was writing (maybe never finished).

questions.prompt: Odjednom je ___ moj brat. (doći, past, m.)
questions.explanation: A sudden plot event → perfective doći: došao je.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'In the end we went home.'
questions.explanation: Na kraju (in the end) + smo (clitic in second position) + otišli kući.

48.title: Phone openers and closers
48.intro: Phone Croatian is wonderfully formulaic: a fixed opener, a fixed closer, and a few set moves in between. Learn these as whole chunks — on the phone there is no body language to help you.

items.note: ovdje = 'here' — the standard way to identify yourself

items.en: Good day, this is Marko Horvat. (formal opener)

items.hr: Mogu li razgovarati s gospođom Kovač?
items.en: May I speak with Mrs Kovač?
He/She is not here at the moment.
Talk to you soon! (lit. 'we'll hear each other')

48.title: On the phone
48.intro: Openers, closers and the moves in between — plus writing a short message.

questions.prompt: Your phone rings. How do you answer?
questions.explanation: Halo? (or Molim?) is the standard way to answer the phone.

questions.prompt: Dobar dan, ___ Ana Kovač. (this is / '...speaking')
questions.explanation: On the phone you identify yourself with 'ovdje + name': Ovdje Ana Kovač.

questions.prompt: What does 'Mogu li razgovarati s gospodinom Horvatom?' mean?
options: May I speak with Mr Horvat?
Can Mr Horvat call me back?
questions.answer: May I speak with Mr Horvat?
questions.explanation: Mogu li + infinitive = May I...; razgovarati s + instrumental = speak with.

questions.prompt: Mogu li ostaviti ___? (a message)
questions.explanation: poruka in the accusative: Mogu li ostaviti poruku?

questions.prompt: Someone calls asking for a person you've never heard of. You say:
questions.explanation: krivi broj = wrong number.

questions.prompt: Nije tu trenutačno? U redu, nazvat ću ___. (later)
questions.explanation: Nazvat ću kasnije = I'll call back later.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'May I speak with Mrs Kovač?'
questions.answer: Mogu li razgovarati s gospođom Kovač?
questions.explanation: Mogu li + infinitive + s + instrumental (gospođom); the surname stays unchanged.

questions.prompt: How do you say goodbye to a friend on the phone?
questions.explanation: Čujemo se! = 'we'll hear each other' — the standard friendly phone sign-off.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I want to reserve a table for two people.'
questions.answer: Želim rezervirati stol za dvije osobe.
questions.explanation: želim + infinitive; za + accusative (dvije osobe).

48.title: Booking a table by phone
48.intro: You call a restaurant in Split to book a table for tonight. Practise until you can do your side without reading — on a real call you won't have subtitles.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Restoran Marjan, izvolite?
lines.en: Restaurant Marjan, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Dobar dan. Želim rezervirati stol za večeras, molim.
lines.en: Good day. I'd like to reserve a table for tonight, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Naravno. Za koliko osoba?
lines.en: Of course. For how many people?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Za dvije osobe, molim.
lines.en: For two people, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: U koliko sati?
lines.en: At what time?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: U osam sati, ako je moguće.
lines.en: At eight o'clock, if possible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Može. Na koje ime?
lines.en: That works. Under what name?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Na ime Horvat. H-O-R-V-A-T.
lines.en: Under the name Horvat. H-O-R-V-A-T.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: U redu. Stol za dvoje večeras u dvadeset sati, na ime Horvat.
lines.en: All right. A table for two tonight at 8 p.m., under the name Horvat.

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Hvala lijepa. Doviđenja!
lines.en: Thank you very much. Goodbye!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hvala Vama. Doviđenja i vidimo se večeras!
lines.en: Thank you. Goodbye, and see you tonight!

49.title: Listening sprint — radio bulletin
49.intro: Today's routine: (1) listen (or read aloud fast) WITHOUT looking at the text, (2) re-listen while reading, (3) shadow one minute closely. Practice transcript — a short radio bulletin: 'Dobro jutro! Ovdje Radio Zagreb. Danas je četvrtak, deveti srpnja. Vrijeme je sunčano, temperatura je dvadeset osam stupnjeva. Autobusi i tramvaji voze normalno, ali vlak za Split kasni dvadeset minuta. Želimo vam ugodan dan!' The phrases below are your survival kit when real-speed Croatian outruns you.

items.note: ask about any word this way

items.en: the train is twenty minutes late
We wish you a pleasant day!

49.title: What did the radio say?
49.intro: Comprehension check on the bulletin, plus your listening survival phrases.

questions.prompt: Koji je dan u radijskim vijestima?
questions.explanation: 'Danas je četvrtak, deveti srpnja.' — Today is Thursday, July 9th.

questions.prompt: Kakvo je vrijeme danas prema vijestima?
questions.explanation: 'Vrijeme je sunčano' — the weather is sunny.

questions.prompt: Vlak za Split ___ dvadeset minuta. (is late)
questions.explanation: kasniti = to be late; vlak kasni dvadeset minuta.

questions.prompt: What does 'Možete li govoriti sporije?' mean?
questions.explanation: sporije = more slowly — your best friend during a listening sprint.

questions.prompt: Oprostite, možete li ___? (repeat)
questions.explanation: ponoviti = to repeat; Možete li ponoviti?

questions.prompt: Kolika je temperatura prema vijestima?
questions.explanation: 'temperatura je dvadeset osam stupnjeva' = 28 degrees.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I didn't understand — can you repeat that?'
questions.explanation: Nisam razumio (f.: razumjela) + možete li + infinitive.

questions.prompt: Autobusi i tramvaji voze ___. (normally)
questions.explanation: voze normalno = are running normally.

50.title: Ten questions about you — answer templates
50.intro: These are the questions every Croatian conversation partner asks in the first five minutes. Build a personal answer for each template now, say it aloud, and time yourself — the goal is an automatic answer within two seconds.

items.en: What is your name? → My name is...
Where are you from? → I am from...

items.hr: Gdje živite? → Živim u Zagrebu, blizu centra.
items.en: Where do you live? → I live in Zagreb, near the centre.

items.hr: Čime se bavite? → Radim kao... / Ja sam student.
items.en: What do you do? → I work as... / I am a student.

items.hr: Koliko dugo učite hrvatski? → Učim hrvatski već sedam tjedana.
items.en: How long have you been learning Croatian? → I've been learning Croatian for seven weeks now.

items.hr: Zašto učite hrvatski? → Jer volim Hrvatsku i želim razgovarati s ljudima.
items.en: Why are you learning Croatian? → Because I love Croatia and want to talk with people.

items.hr: Što volite raditi u slobodno vrijeme? → Volim čitati, kuhati i šetati.
items.en: What do you like doing in your free time? → I like reading, cooking and walking.

items.hr: Što ste radili jučer? → Jučer sam radio, a navečer sam učio hrvatski.
items.en: What did you do yesterday? → Yesterday I worked, and in the evening I studied Croatian.

items.hr: Kakvi su vaši planovi za vikend? → Za vikend planiram putovati u Split.
items.en: What are your plans for the weekend? → For the weekend I plan to travel to Split.

items.hr: Recite mi nešto o svojoj obitelji. → Imam brata i sestru.
items.en: Tell me something about your family. → I have a brother and a sister.

50.title: Interview warm-up
50.intro: Sharpen the building blocks of your answers before the mock interview below.

questions.prompt: 'Čime se bavite?' asks about your:
questions.explanation: Čime se bavite? (lit. 'with what do you occupy yourself?') = What do you do for a living?

questions.prompt: Kako se zovete? — "___ se Ana." (I am called)
questions.explanation: zvati se: Zovem se Ana = My name is Ana.

questions.prompt: Učim hrvatski ___ sedam tjedana. (already / for ... now)
questions.explanation: već + present tense = 'have been ...ing for': Učim već sedam tjedana.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I live in Zagreb, near the centre.'
questions.explanation: u + locative (Zagrebu); blizu + genitive (centra).

questions.prompt: Best answer to 'Zašto učite hrvatski?':
questions.explanation: zašto → jer: Why → Because I want to talk with people.

questions.prompt: Za vikend ___ putovati u Split. (planirati, ja)
questions.explanation: planirati + infinitive: planiram putovati = I plan to travel.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'What do you like doing in your free time?'
questions.answer: Što volite raditi u slobodno vrijeme?
questions.explanation: Što + volite + infinitive + u slobodno vrijeme (accusative).

questions.prompt: 'Odakle ste?' — which is a correct answer?
questions.explanation: odakle → iz + genitive: iz Amerike = from America.

questions.prompt: Želim razgovarati s ___. (ljudi — people, instrumental)
questions.explanation: ljudi → ljudima in the instrumental plural: razgovarati s ljudima.

50.title: Mock interview — ten questions about your life
50.intro: The final rehearsal of Phase 1: a partner interviews you with the highest-frequency personal questions. The Vi lines are templates — replace the details with YOUR facts and answer each question aloud within two seconds, without reading.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dobar dan! Kako se zovete?
lines.en: Good day! What is your name?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Dobar dan! Zovem se Ana Smith.
lines.en: Good day! My name is Ana Smith.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odakle ste?
lines.en: Where are you from?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Ja sam iz Amerike, iz Chicaga.
lines.en: I am from America, from Chicago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gdje sada živite?
lines.en: Where do you live now?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Živim u Zagrebu, u malom stanu blizu centra.
lines.en: I live in Zagreb, in a small flat near the centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Čime se bavite?
lines.en: What do you do for a living?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Radim kao programerka. Posao mi je zanimljiv.
lines.en: I work as a programmer. My job is interesting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Koliko dugo učite hrvatski?
lines.en: How long have you been learning Croatian?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Učim hrvatski već sedam tjedana, svaki dan po sat vremena.
lines.en: I've been learning Croatian for seven weeks now, an hour every day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Zašto učite hrvatski?
lines.en: Why are you learning Croatian?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Jer volim Hrvatsku i želim razgovarati s ljudima na hrvatskom.
lines.en: Because I love Croatia and want to talk with people in Croatian.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Što volite raditi u slobodno vrijeme?
lines.en: What do you like doing in your free time?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Volim čitati, kuhati i šetati uz more.
lines.en: I like reading, cooking and walking along the sea.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Što ste radili jučer?
lines.en: What did you do yesterday?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Jučer sam radila, a navečer sam gledala film i učila hrvatski.
lines.en: Yesterday I worked, and in the evening I watched a film and studied Croatian.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kakvi su vaši planovi za vikend?
lines.en: What are your plans for the weekend?

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Za vikend planiram putovati vlakom u Split s prijateljima.
lines.en: For the weekend I plan to travel to Split by train with friends.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Odlično govorite hrvatski! Hvala vam na razgovoru.
lines.en: You speak Croatian excellently! Thank you for the conversation.

lines.speaker: Vi
lines.hr: Hvala lijepa! Bilo mi je drago. Doviđenja!
lines.en: Thank you very much! It was my pleasure. Goodbye!
